title_tag: Nebraska Worker Classification State Test 2026 | Teamed
meta_description: Nebraska worker classification 2026: IRS 20-factor common-law test for state tax, NDOL 10-factor right-to-control test (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-604(5)) for UI, Employee Classification Act (§ 48-2701) for construction and delivery. No state ABC test.
target_keywords: neb rev stat 48-2701 employee classification act
primary_tests_covered: IRS 20-factor common-law test (state income tax + workers' comp)
NDOL 10-factor right-to-control test (unemployment insurance, Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-604(5))
Employee Classification Act (construction + delivery, Neb. Rev. Stat. §§ 48-2701 to 48-2716)
key_statutes: Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-604(5) — NDOL UI 10-factor right-to-control test
Neb. Rev. Stat. §§ 48-2701 to 48-2716 — Employee Classification Act (construction + delivery)
Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-2703 — IC criteria under ECA
Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-2705 — employee presumption in covered sectors
Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-2710 — Class III civil violation
Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-2712 — criminal misdemeanour for wilful misclassification
Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-2713 — NDOL interagency coordination

flags.detail: No dollar-per-day penalty figure exists in the ECA statute equivalent to Illinois's $1,500/day figure. ECA defines violation as Class III; fine schedule set by NDOL rulemaking. Page correctly identifies violation class without asserting a specific per-worker-per-day figure. PROVISIONAL — verify exact penalty schedule with NDOL before client-specific advice.

The 10-factor enumeration is assembled from Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-604(5) and standard NDOL right-to-control guidance. Verify the exact NDOL factor list against current NDOL audit guidance before using in a client determination.

No Meeting Intelligence quote available for Nebraska-specific worker classification analysis. Pullquote anonymised to 'Teamed Legal Operations' per spec. No fabricated cite line. Rule applied: pullquote-provenance rule (2026-05-22).
